title: VimFu — Ctrl-Y
description: Ctrl-Y scrolls the window one line down (viewport moves up). The cursor stays in place. Opposite of Ctrl-E.

setup.comment: Set TERM for proper color support in Neovim

setup.content:
# Pizza Order System

toppings = [
    "pepperoni",
    "mushrooms",
    "onions",
    "sausage",
    "bell pepper",
    "olives",
    "pineapple",
]

sizes = {
    "small": 8.99,
    "medium": 12.99,
    "large": 15.99,
    "xl": 19.99,
}

def calculate_price(size, extras):
    base = sizes[size]
    extra_cost = len(extras) * 1.50
    return base + extra_cost

def format_order(name, size, tops):
    total = calculate_price(size, tops)
    print(f"Order for {name}")
    print(f"Size: {size}")
    for t in tops:
        print(f"  + {t}")
    print(f"Total: ${total:.2f}")

setup.comment: Launch nvim at bottom so we can scroll up

steps.say: Control Y scrolls the window one line up. The viewport shifts but the cursor stays where it is.

steps.comment: --- Demo 1: scroll up a few lines ---
Move cursor to middle of screen so scroll is visible

steps.say: The cursor is in the middle. Press control Y three times.
Three more lines appeared at the top. The cursor stayed right where it was.
Use a count. Five control Y scrolls five lines.
Five more lines revealed. We can see the toppings list now.
Control Y scrolls up. Control E scrolls down. One line at a time, precise viewport control.
